item.title: The Thrill of Childhood Rituals
item.description: The joy of playing tag and creating secret languages has persisted across time and place.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: The Iran War Is Putting Pressure on Europe
item.description: Panelists joined to discuss the effects of the conflict on America’s allies, and more.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: The Romance of the Gas-Station Sign
item.description: A few glowing roadside numerals set the national mood.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: America Has a New GLP-1 Playbook
item.description: First a shot to lose weight, then a pill to keep it off.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Florko

item.title: Iran Could Speedrun the Vietnamese Path
item.dc:creator: Graeme Wood

item.title: Iran Has the Upper Hand in the Upcoming Negotiations
item.description: Turning the cease-fire into a longer stalemate is a matter of political will.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Why Trump Chose Vance to Negotiate With Iran
item.description: There is no overlap between what America is demanding and what Iran is offering.
item.dc:creator: Karim Sadjadpour

item.title: Steven Soderbergh’s Strange, Surprising Heist Movie
item.description: The Christophersstarts as the tale of a forgery, but ends as an intimate meditation on art.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Iran Out-Trolled the Troller in Chief
item.description: The war sparked an epic social-media trolling contest.
item.dc:creator: Ali Breland

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Soccer Stars
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Writers Who Can’t Let Go of the South
item.description: Southern experiences and traditions can be deeply compelling, even exotic, to Americans who live in other areas.
item.dc:creator: Emma Sarappo

item.title: How Fake People Became Real Influencers
item.description: AI avatars are redefining influence and trust online.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: Viktor Orbán Could Actually Lose
item.description: The Hungarian leader faces an energized opposition—and questions about whether he would accept defeat.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: How Did Samuel Alito Become This Angry?
item.description: A quiet, bookish justice’s personal leanings have become ever more overt.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Canellos

item.title: A Different Moon From the One We’ve Known
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission is bringing home a view of the moon unlike any humanity has seen before.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boyle

item.title: The Iran War Showed a New Side of Pope Leo
item.description: Don’t call him the quiet pope anymore.
item.dc:creator: Francis X. Rocca

item.title: Photos of the Week: Solar Eclipse, Amazon Monument, Titanic Bonfire
item.description: Blossoming cherry trees in Germany, a fertility and harvest festival in Poland, a horse harness parade in England, a Tomb-Sweeping Day festival in Hong Kong, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Great Travel Meltdown of 2026
item.description: Airports are suffering a perfect storm of actual problems and passenger anxieties.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: The Banality of MAGA-fication
item.description: A new book by an unremarkable Republican accidentally illuminates the devolution of the party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Is Schoolwork Optional Now?
item.description: Education is on the verge of becoming fully automated.
item.dc:creator: Lila Shroff
